game:charattribute-wholeVesselLootChance: 30% chance to collect cracked vessels intact
spinningwheel:handbook-spinningwheel-tex:
Spinning Twine

Spinning twine can be done using a drop spindle or a spinning wheel. The drop spindle can be used while traveling, but is slower and requires 4 fibers for each twine. The spinning wheel is faster and uses 2 fibers for each twine, but it can only be used by Tailors, and requires them to be stationary.

How to Use the Spinning Wheel

1. Place the spinning wheel
The spinning wheel is a 2x2x2 multiblock structure. Place it on a flat surface with enough clearance.

2. Sit down
Right-click the spinning wheel to sit at it. Your character will take a seat at the wheel, ready to spin.

3. Open the crafting menu
While seated, right click any part to open the spinning wheel interface. This allows you to load fibers and collect your output.

4. Add fibers
Place spinnable fibers (such as flax fibers) into the input slot.

5. Spin
Once seated with fibers loaded, your character will automatically begin spinning. The wheel will turn and fibers will be converted into twine over time.

6. Collect output
Remove the finished twine from the output slot when spinning is complete.
spinningwheel:handbook-loom-text:
Creating Linen

Linen can be created from twine by knitting. The flying shuttle loom is an advanced weaving station that converts twine into linen, only usable by Tailors. Named after its shuttle mechanism that flies back and forth across the warp threads, the loom provides an immersive way to create fabric.

How to Use the Loom

1. Place the loom
The loom is a 3x2x2 multiblock structure. Ensure you have adequate space before placing it.

2. Sit down
Right-click the loom to sit at it. Your character will position themselves at the weaving bench.

3. Open the weaving menu
While seated, right click any part to open the loom interface.

4. Load twine
Place flax twine into the input slot.

5. Weave
Once seated with twine loaded, your character will begin weaving. You will see animated shuttle movement as the cloth is produced.

6. Collect cloth
Remove the finished cloth from the output slot.

Tips
- Knitting uses 4 flax twine for each linen.
- Knitting takes 4 seconds per linen, or 3 seconds if sitting.
- The flying shuttle loom creates 3 linen from 9 twine at a time.
knitting:handbook-needles-text: Can be used to knit 4 twine into 1 linen.
xskills:abilitydesc-fastpotter: You have a chance each time you add or remove clay to instantly finish the pottery piece. The base chance is {0}%, plus 0.{1}% per pottery skill level, up to a maximum of {2}% per step. The chance is lower at first, but increases toward the maximum the further along the work already is.
xskills:abilitydesc-finishingtouch: You have a chance for every hit to instantly finish the smithing work. The base chance is {0}%, plus 0.{1}% per metalworking skill level, up to a maximum of {2}% per hit. The chance is lower at first, but increases toward the maximum the further along the work already is.
rpvoicechat:starterguide-text:
RP Voice Chat - Starter Guide
Shout at your enemies, talk with your friends, conspire with your allies.

This mod implements VoIP to allow people on a server together to talk using microphones in-game based on proximity. It includes also a bunch of in-game communication tools !

About VoIP
To open the configuration menu you can press the keybind which by default is set to "Shift + Tab". Make sure to double check the keybinds. They're under user interface and have the prefix "RPVoice".
You cycle between the following speech levels with a keybind defined in your settings.

These dictate how far your voice can be heard.
Whispering: Reaches max 5 blocks
Talking: Reaches max 15 blocks
Shouting: Reaches max 25 blocks

The above distances can be set per world using chat commands. Using these commands require server control privileges.
The commands are all subcommands of "/rpvc" so just use "/help rpvc" and you should get more information.

Communication tools
RP Voice Chat includes more than VoIP. You will find items and blocks that will give you new ways to communicate. For example :
Horns
Bells
Megaphones
Lights
Telegraph
Telephone
